Manipulated right edge due to unrepresentative calculation of right edge during zero flow conditions. This measurement should be reviewed by a supervisor. Purged o-line at 14:07. HG within 0.003m after purge. No reset. ADCP by Moving Boat  AK 2023-10-19 - AFTER REVIEWING MEASUREMENT IN OFFICE, THE RIGHT EDGE NEEDED FURTHER ADJUSTMENT. CHANGED EDGE CALCULATION TO RECTANGULAR FROM TRIANGULAR  SEE "10CD001_20231015_M1R.pdf" FOR ADDITIONAL DETAILS.  Two transects were done in WinRiver II, and the River Right edge was estimated at 40m.  With such a large edge estimate,  the edge equaled about 18% of flow, which seemed odd to the tech since there was very slow flow at this point and they had problems controlling the boat.  In Qrev, the tech made the first revision, and made the River Right edge (type: triangle) to 2m (which made the edge about 1.1% of flow).  In Qrev (in the revised file) AK made a second revision, and made the River Right edge to 'Rectangle' which was intended to cut the remaining 38m of zero flow out of the measurement.

column14: Tethered boat RR measurement was a little tricky. Right edge was ~30m and still going from 8 bins to 0... Hard to control boat d/s of bridge pier, even with added throw rope regular rope. No MBT done as we did transects first and then lost connection, already running late, already planning to use GPS as there is assumed to be moving bed. Did 4 transects but ended up using only first 2 with VTG reference as these matched the closest with the fewest errors.  Reset HG to 1.621m at 14:42pst. ADCP by Moving Boat

column14: POST MMT DWL NOT CHECKED FOR MGH. UPDATED MGH FROM -0.189 TO -0.191 - RV2021.10.18  No GPS today. Moving bed found, using BT with moving bed correction. Corrected right edge for transect 1 from 55m to 35m (typo). Third transect was us pulling tethered boat back to shore. Due to the wide edge, we wanted to ensure there wasn't a major change in depth/flow that was being missed...nothing evident. High %, but shift is comparable to May and June mmts here.  Moved existing anchor at 8:00, delete for maintenance until 8:40. Reset applied for new anchor extension at 8:42, data valid again 8:45. Suspect pressure was still equalizing. Re-applied offset at 10:08 before leaving site. ADCP by Moving Boat

The ADCP head was 38 minutes, and 50 seconds too slow (e.g. ADCP time : 15:11:13, and actually time was 15:50:03).   There is an eddy line on the left edge of River. The discharge on the Left edge can vary depending if the tethered boat floats into the eddy line or not. Q of left edge is not significant Sources:eHSN v1.2.4
